evidence.text: pe-hub-east-1 connects 4 branches and 1 DC over MPLS. Uplink eth3 to p-core-1 is 40G.
Step 6. Apply a soft pre-emptive reroute by raising the TE cost of the affected link by 1000. Step 8. Rollback: revert the TE cost.

root_cause_hypothesis.summary: The pe-hub-east-1 uplink eth3 to p-core-1 is rising toward saturation (71.4% and trending up). Combined with rising AF21 queue depth, voice and video classes will start dropping within ~22 minutes if the trend continues.

evidence_chunks.quote: pe-hub-east-1 connects 4 branches and 1 DC over MPLS. Uplink eth3 to p-core-1 is 40G.
evidence_chunks.relevance: Identifies the affected link and its capacity.

evidence_chunks.quote: Apply a soft pre-emptive reroute by raising the TE cost of the affected link by 1000.

recommended_actions.expected_effect: Offload ~15-25% of traffic from the saturated link; queue depth on AF21 should fall within 5 min.
recommended_actions.rollback: no mpls traffic-eng interface eth3 metric 2000

recommended_actions.expected_effect: Capture per-class drops so we can confirm the impact in 10 min.

operator_questions.q1_what_will_fail: The pe-hub-east-1 uplink eth3 to p-core-1 is forecast to saturate in ~22 minutes, dropping voice and video on cust-blue.
operator_questions.q2_why_elevated_risk: if_out_util_pct on eth3 is 71.4% and rising; AF21-data queue depth is 482000 bytes and rising. The ensemble model has 0.78 confidence based on the recent 18-minute trend.
operator_questions.q3_corrective_action: Open a change ticket, then raise the TE cost on pe-hub-east-1 eth3 by 1000 per MPLS-003 step 6. Monitor AF21 drops for 10 minutes; rollback with the inverse command if traffic shifts are unexpected.

evidence.text: Step 1. Identify the underlay link using the affected P/PE loopbacks. Step 6. Apply a soft pre-emptive reroute by raising the TE cost of the affected link by 1000.
Root cause: a fibre splice in the east ring degraded over several days, producing CRC errors that gradually exceeded the link's FEC correction capacity.

root_cause_hypothesis.summary: Rising CRC errors on pe-hub-east-1 eth1, falling LDP holdtime, and rising jitter on the sdwan-nyc-sfo tunnel are a textbook precursor pattern for an MPLS underlay fibre degradation. The most recent comparable incident (INC-2026-03-14-001) was a degraded fibre splice on the same hub-east ring.

evidence_chunks.quote: Apply a soft pre-emptive reroute by raising the TE cost of the affected link by 1000.
a fibre splice in the east ring degraded over several days, producing CRC errors
evidence_chunks.relevance: Prior incident with the same signal pattern.

recommended_actions.expected_effect: Captures the change in the audit log and pages the on-call if the reroute is rolled back automatically.
recommended_actions.rollback: close ticket after reroute is reverted

recommended_actions.expected_effect: Offload traffic from the degrading link; voice impact should clear within 90 s per INC-2026-03-14-001.
recommended_actions.rollback: no mpls traffic-eng interface eth1 metric 2000

recommended_actions.expected_effect: Dispatch fibre OTDR test for the east ring within 30 min.
recommended_actions.rollback: acknowledge page if test is cancelled

operator_questions.q1_what_will_fail: The pe-hub-east-1 ↔ p-core-1 underlay will exceed 2% loss in ~15 minutes, dropping voice on the sdwan-nyc-sfo tunnel.
operator_questions.q2_why_elevated_risk: CRC error rate on eth1 is 28.4/s and rising; LDP holdtime is dropping from 15s to 11s; tunnel jitter p95 is 12ms and rising. INC-2026-03-14-001 was the same signal pattern on the same ring.
operator_questions.q3_corrective_action: Open a change ticket, raise the TE cost on pe-hub-east-1 eth1 by 1000 per MPLS-003, and page facilities to dispatch an OTDR for the east ring.
